query: How does the project keep all data private and avoid using cloud AI services?
rationale: HLD 'Design Goals > Fully Local' (no cloud, privacy) and PRD 'Vision' (No external AI APIs, no cloud storage). Query uses none of the titles.

query: Why was FastAPI chosen for the backend?
rationale: HLD 'Design Decisions > Why FastAPI' (Python-first AI ecosystem, REST). Title 'High Level Design' absent from query.

query: What made ChromaDB a good fit for the MVP vector store?
rationale: HLD 'Design Decisions > Why ChromaDB' (lightweight, easy setup, docker support).

query: materialized read model to avoid complex joins
rationale: Conga doc 'KEY FINDINGS' and 'KEY TERMS' explicitly mention a materialized read model / denormalization. Term appears in body, not title.
Exact phrase in Conga doc describing how a lookup name update reflects in referenced objects.

query: removing unused exports from the bundle with tree shaking
rationale: Webpack doc 'Tree Shaking' section (removes unused exports, sideEffects flag). Body term, not title.

query: Kahn's algorithm using an indegree array and a queue
rationale: Topological Sort doc describes Kahn's Algorithm with indegree array and queue. Terms in body, title is just 'Topological Sort'.

query: harness decay and the build to delete philosophy
rationale: Harness Engineering doc 'BRUTAL TRUTH ABOUT HARNESSES' covers Harness Decay and the Build to Delete philosophy.

query: using uv with pyproject.toml and a lockfile for isolated environments
rationale: Python Environments doc covers uv, pyproject.toml, uv.lock, venv isolation.

query: how do I detect a cycle in a directed graph?
rationale: Both 'DFS Cycle Detection' (recursion stack / back edge) and 'Topological Sort' (Kahn's cycle detection) answer this. Recall across two docs.

query: different strategies for splitting documents into smaller pieces before embedding
rationale: 'Technical Details on Chunking and other strategies' lists fixed-size, semantic, recursive, adaptive, context-enriched, AI-driven chunking.
